title: Forcepoint Web Security On-Premises URL Blocks (`websense:cg:kv`)
dataSources: Forcepoint Web Security / Websense proxy syslog key-value logs, `sourcetype=websense:cg:kv`
app: Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security (Splunkbase 2966)
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="websense:cg:kv" earliest=-7d
| eval act=lower(coalesce(action, disposition, result))
| where match(act,"(?i)block|deny|drop")
| stats count by user, url, category, policy
| sort -count
| head 40
description: Traditional Forcepoint Web Security appliances still enforce acceptable-use and threat categories for many enterprises. Blocked URL telemetry validates policy strictness, identifies compromised accounts generating unusual categories, and supports HR or insider investigations.
value: Maintains parity between legacy on-prem web proxies and newer SSE dashboards inside a single Splunk search head.
implementation: Forward appliance syslog to Splunk with the vendor-recommended KV format. Install Splunkbase 2966 for CIM Web mappings. Map `category` to internal risk tiers. Deprecation planning: migrate searches to Forcepoint ONE (`forcepoint:one`) as appliances are replaced.
visualization: Table (user, URL category), Bar chart (blocks by category), Timeline.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Source types for Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security
Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security
